speaker: CAESAR
text: Let him appear that’s come from Antony.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Know you him?

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: Caesar, ’tis his schoolmaster—

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: An argument that he is plucked, when hither

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: He sends so poor a pinion of his wing,

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: Which had superfluous kings for messengers

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: Not many moons gone by.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Approach, and speak.

speaker: AMBASSADOR
text: Such as I am, I come from Antony.

speaker: AMBASSADOR
text: I was of late as petty to his ends

speaker: AMBASSADOR
text: As is the morn-dew on the myrtle leaf

speaker: AMBASSADOR
text: To his grand sea.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Be ’t so. Declare thine office.

speaker: AMBASSADOR
text: Lord of his fortunes he salutes thee, and

speaker: AMBASSADOR
text: Requires to live in Egypt, which not granted,

speaker: AMBASSADOR
text: He lessens his requests, and to thee sues

speaker: AMBASSADOR
text: To let him breathe between the heavens and Earth,

speaker: AMBASSADOR
text: A private man in Athens. This for him.

speaker: AMBASSADOR
text: Next, Cleopatra does confess thy greatness,

speaker: AMBASSADOR
text: Submits her to thy might, and of thee craves

speaker: AMBASSADOR
text: The circle of the Ptolemies for her heirs,

speaker: AMBASSADOR
text: Now hazarded to thy grace.

speaker: CAESAR
text: For Antony,

speaker: CAESAR
text: I have no ears to his request. The Queen

speaker: CAESAR
text: Of audience nor desire shall fail, so she

speaker: CAESAR
text: From Egypt drive her all-disgracèd friend,

speaker: CAESAR
text: Or take his life there. This if she perform,

speaker: CAESAR
text: She shall not sue unheard. So to them both.

speaker: AMBASSADOR
text: Fortune pursue thee!

speaker: CAESAR
text: Bring him through the bands.

speaker: CAESAR
text:
To
                 
                Thidias
                .
                 
              To try thy eloquence now ’tis time.Dispatch.

speaker: CAESAR
text: From Antony win Cleopatra. Promise,

speaker: CAESAR
text: And in our name, what she requires; add more,

speaker: CAESAR
text: From thine invention, offers. Women are not

speaker: CAESAR
text: In their best fortunes strong, but want will perjure

speaker: CAESAR
text: The ne’er-touched vestal. Try thy cunning, Thidias.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Make thine own edict for thy pains, which we

speaker: CAESAR
text: Will answer as a law.

speaker: THIDIAS
text: Caesar, I go.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Observe how Antony becomes his flaw,

speaker: CAESAR
text: And what thou think’st his very action speaks

speaker: CAESAR
text: In every power that moves.

speaker: THIDIAS
text: Caesar, I shall.
